title: 3 Low-Volatility ETFs for Peace of Mind in Turbulent Times
author: Nathan Reiff
description: Amid geopolitical tensions affecting oil prices and market volatility, investors seeking stability can consider low-volatility ETFs. The article highlights three options: LVHI (Franklin International Low Volatility High Dividend Index ETF) offering 12% YTD returns with 4.1% dividend yield; JEPI (JPMorgan Equity Premium Income ETF) providing 8.3% dividend yield through options strategies; and IEF (iShares 7-10 Year Treasury Bond ETF) offering 3.8% yield with lower risk exposure.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong YTD performance of 12% combined with attractive 4.1% dividend yield and low 0.4% expense ratio makes it a compelling choice for income-focused, risk-averse investors seeking international diversification.

Actively managed fund with impressive 8.3% dividend yield achieved through options overlay strategy, competitive 0.35% fee, and outperformance of S&P 500 YTD despite conservative positioning appeals to income-seeking investors.

Offers balanced approach with 3.8% dividend yield, very low 0.15% expense ratio, and intermediate-term Treasury exposure providing middle ground between yield and interest rate risk for defensive portfolios.
Presented as alternative for risk-minimizing investors with similar 0.15% expense ratio and 3.7% dividend yield, providing shorter-duration Treasury exposure with reduced interest rate sensitivity.

title: The Oil Shock Hit. The Treasury Shock Is Coming, This Chart Warns
author: Piero Cingari
description: While WTI crude rallied to $99, technical analyst John Roque warns that the 2-Year Treasury yield at 3.92% is significantly underpriced relative to historical levels. He targets a 5% yield, which would be roughly 100 basis points higher and would effectively kill expectations of Fed rate cuts in 2026. A longer-term risk involves a potential massive technical base forming around 5.20%, which could trigger a major repricing of the entire bond market and impact rate-sensitive sectors.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The ETF would decline as short-dated Treasury yields rise. The article warns of a potential 100+ basis point increase in 2-Year yields, which would directly harm the value of short-duration bond holdings.

title: AdvisorNet Financial Cuts $3.9 Million From Short-Term Treasury ETF — Here's What Long-Term Investors Should Know
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: AdvisorNet Financial sold approximately 47,100 shares of the iShares 1-3 Year Treasury Bond ETF (SHY) in Q3, reducing its position by $3.9 million while maintaining SHY as 0.8% of its reportable assets.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The ETF maintains stable characteristics with a 3.88% yield, low volatility, and continues to serve as a reliable fixed-income investment option

title: Are Investors Selling US Treasuries As Trump Attacks Powell? Here's What Latest ETF Flows Tell You
author: Piero Cingari
description: Trump's attacks on Fed Chair Powell have led to concerns about the politicization of the Federal Reserve, causing a rare simultaneous selloff in stocks, Treasuries, and the dollar. However, ETF flows suggest no broad investor flight from the Treasury market, with a tilt towards shorter maturities likely reflecting a desire to hedge against rate volatility and political uncertainty.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The ETF has seen a modest recovery in inflows after a large outflow in March, suggesting no broad exodus from the Treasury market.
The ETF has seen strong inflows, indicating investor preference for shorter-duration Treasuries to hedge against rate volatility and political uncertainty.
The ETF has seen the highest inflows since July 2022, further suggesting a shift towards intermediate-term Treasuries.
The ETF has seen the best monthly inflows since February 2023, indicating investor demand for short-term Treasuries.
The ETF has seen the strongest inflows since June 2024, reflecting investor preference for intermediate-term Treasuries.

title: Prices Decline In May, Monetary Policy Tightens
author: American Institute For Economic Research
description: The United States experienced a mild deflation in May, according to new data from the Bureau of Economic Analysis. Click to read.
